// This package MUST NOT COMPILE.
//
// It is the proof for the enforcer-isolation requirement. The claim is that an
// enforcer *cannot* see classifier internals — and the only faithful test of
// "cannot" is a compilation that cannot succeed. A lint rule would test "does
// not today"; a comment would test nothing.
//
// Stored as .txt so the normal build ignores it; compile_test.go copies it into
// a temp module and asserts the build fails with the expected error.
package enforcerisolation

import (
	"context"

	"github.com/lucianoengel/openshield/internal/core"
	corev1 "github.com/lucianoengel/openshield/internal/core/corev1"
)

// leakyEnforcer tries to receive classifier output alongside the Decision.
type leakyEnforcer struct{}

func (leakyEnforcer) Capabilities() []corev1.Action {
	return []corev1.Action{corev1.Action_ACTION_ALERT}
}

// Enforce takes the Classification — exactly what the contract forbids.
func (leakyEnforcer) Enforce(ctx context.Context, d *corev1.Decision, c *corev1.LocalClassification) error {
	_ = c.GetMatches()[0].GetMatchedText() // the leak this design prevents
	return nil
}

// This assertion is what fails: leakyEnforcer does not satisfy core.Enforcer,
// because Enforcer.Enforce accepts a Decision and nothing else.
var _ core.Enforcer = (*leakyEnforcer)(nil)
